title: Golden Toaster Oven Yellow Cake
ingredients: 1 stick Unsalted Butter (melted and cooled slightly)
directions: Preheat your toaster oven using Convection Bake setting to 325°F.
Rest your flexible silicone cake pan directly onto the solid metal baking sheet.
In a large cauldron bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a second bowl, beat your eggs (or activate egg-free substitute spell!), milk, vanilla, and melted butter.
Pour liquids into dry and fold slowly with a spatula until flour disappears. Do not overmix!
Pour the batter into the silicone pan, leveling the top.
Bake for 25 minutes on the middle rack until a toothpick plunged in the center emerges dry.
Let cool completely on the counter for at least 30 minutes.

title: Creamy Buttercream Spooky Frosting
ingredients: 1 stick Unsalted Butter (fully softened to room temperature)
3 tbsp Heavy Whipping Cream (or Half & Half)
directions: In a clean mixing bowl, beat the softened butter with a sturdy spoon or hand mixer for 1 full minute until smooth.
Slowly stir in about half of the powdered sugar until combined.
Pour in the vanilla flavor and 2 tablespoons of the heavy whipping cream. Stir until smooth.
Slowly add the remaining powdered sugar and whip well.
Check texture: if too dry, add a tiny splash more cream; if too soft, add another spoonful of powdered sugar.
Spread evenly onto your yellow cake.

title: Toxic Mint Whipped Dream
directions: Chill your bowl and whisk in the freezer for 10 minutes prior to whisking.
Pour the cold heavy whipping cream into the chilled bowl.
Whip vigorously until the cream starts holding shape.
Add the powdered sugar and vanilla flavor.
Whip just until stiff peaks form. Refrigerate until ready to use.

title: Alchemist Custard Pudding
directions: Gently heat the whole milk in a saucepan until hot, but not boiling.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the sugar, flour, and eggs (or choose egg-free substitute).
Temper the eggs by pouring a very small splash of hot milk into the egg bowl while whisking rapidly.
Pour everything back into the hot pot.
Heat over medium-low, whisking constantly until the mixture bubbles and thickens.
Remove from heat, whisk in the vanilla, and cool before serving.

title: Witching Hours Fluffy Pancakes
directions: Whisk the dry flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt together in a bowl.
Pour in the milk, egg (or egg replacement), and melted butter.
Mix gently. Let the batter rest for 5 minutes (this activates the baking powder).
Ladle portions onto a greased pan over medium-high heat.
Flip once bubble indicators burst on top, cooking until both sides are golden.
